title: 2012 Cervélo S5
description: I am part of the McGill Cycling Team and I went training with my boyfriend Sunday September 23, 2012 in the afternoon. At the end of the ride, we stopped at 3:05 pm at Marche J V T 181 Sherbrooke O, Montral, H2X 1X4, 514-284-4404. My boyfriend went inside the store to make a purchase. I was planning to go straight home since I had to use the washroom but I decided to turn back and wait outside to watch his bike that was inside the front entrance of the store. The store has two entrances and his bike was in the first doorway. After waiting a few minutes, I brought my bike into the first doorway entrance and went inside to tell him to hurry up. I was only 2 meters away from my bike and I could see it through the glass doors. There was surveillance video of the man and you can see that within 7 seconds, the man grabbed my bike (Cervelo S5) and rode off with it. My boyfriend and I rushed through the entrance doors to chase after the man but he was already biking away too fast. The bike is size 51 cm (relatively small), made of full carbon fiber, Dura-Ace components, Fulcrum wheels, Specialized saddle, Shimano clipless pedals, 1 water bottle cage and 1 water bottle. The bike is glossay black, with red, white and blue accents on top tube and fork. The bike frame has a distinctive wide, aerodynamic frame with white, bold letters written on downtube with "Cervelo" and "S5". The serial number is SNS5C12A00609. The man who stole my bike is possible of native background, had neck-length black, greasy hair, around 35-45 years old, approximately 160lb, 5'6-5'8 tall and walks with a distinctive way of this arms. He was wearing a black hoodie and black track pants with white running shoes. He rode off with my bike up Avenue du Parc and through the first alley way beside Quality Hotel. I called the police immediately and tried looking all over for him and my bike but no luck. I would really like to find the guy and my bike so I can continue training on the McGill Cycling team and get justice for what happened.

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

title: 1994 Specialized Crossroads
description:
I came outside this morning 12/7/24 to find my prized commuter had disappeared from my backyard. Near City Park and Mountain. Likely stolen in the night. It was a barn find that I rebuilt and added many non-stock parts parts to, and I haven't seen seen anything similar to it around town. Unfortunately I did not write down the serial number, but I will be filing reports with Bike Index and the city.

~1994 Red Specialized Crossroads in XL/Large with White Decals

UPANBIKE butterfly bars Wrapped in Red (Most Distinctive)
Black Avid Speed Dial Brake Levers
Koski V-Brake front/ Avid Single Digit Rear Calipers
No-name 3x8 Speed Shifters
Shimano Deore LX Rear Derailleur
Vintage Shimano Exage? Front Derailleur
Puroma Red Pedals
Black Easton Front Wheel - Specialized Sawtooth Tire
Dura-Ace Hub With Mavic Rim Rear Wheel - Goodyear Transit Speed Tire
Silver Sunrace 11-40t Cassette on the Rear Wheel (Big Gear on back wheel)
Bontrager Seat

Other Stuff:

Faded Brown REI Flash Pannier on left side
Jim Blackburn Bottle Cage and JBlackburn Rear Rack
Adjustable Bottle Cage on seat tube
RockBros Tubetop Bag and Frame Bag
Polar Colorado Water Bottle
Red Phone Mount
Blitzu Bike Light (Paint Heavily Faded)
OnGuard U Lock (Unlocked)

title: 2023 Aventón Step-through
description:
Level.2 Step-Through Commuter Ebike, the color is white (officially called "polar"). It has a black metal basket on the back, and a red bell and a phone holder in the front (but who knows what the thieves might detach). The display is scratched and covered with transparent tape. The bike has about 3,000 miles and I've been driving it for >220 hours (according to the display as far as I remember). I added reflectors to the spokes.

The bike trailer (also stolen along with the bike) is Burley Honey Bee and can also be turned into a stroller. It's red, also has reflectors on the spikes, and the tires are Schwalbe Marathon Plus. Inside in the front, the bottom was ripped and fixed with black roof tape from the outside bottom, with a little whole down in the middle.

title: 1994 Trek 1400
description: NARRATIVEWHITE TREK road frame with "1400 BONDED ALUMINUM TREK" written in light blue with light purple shading on bicycle was in back of truck completely enclosed and under truck topper. Thief left the back window hatch to the topper up and tailgate down and did not take brand new pressure washer next to it. Because truck glass is dark tented they had to really peer into the back of truck topper bed windows to see bike lying flat or know it was there from watching. Theft occurred overnight wed may 8,2014.The white bike frame has the shifters on the downtube (older style). It has light blue tape on the curved down racing handle bars tape AND on the triathlon front bars to match the TREK 1400 bonded aluminum written on light blue decals. The LIGHT BLUE MATCHING michelin Kromion tires on Aurora matrix rims 150 c-11 700c are decaled too and have 36 holes. Shimano 105 derailer & componets also have FH-1055 labels. The fork has label "epoxy bonded alumimum alloy" on one side of the fork and the other side of the fork has decal with no writing because I accidently cleaned writing off just one side of the fork with acetone. Easton E9 program 600 series alloy is labeled. 51 inch seat tube vertical and 52 inch top tube horizontal. Black leather Italian seat with long front & white measuring tapes decal on edges of seat for perfect adjustments. Back touring black rack. Triatholan bars have neon yellow on front tips in plastic. brand new hyperglide HG 30 7 speed 11x28 back cog cassette 11-13-15-18-21-24-28 teeth barely ridden. Weights 23.6 pounds without tribars and back bag containing 2 spare tires, yellow lever to change tire, and two air cartridges to fill tire. Petals clipless and flat for normal shoes other side of petal. Brand new non-shimano chain.Dozens of pictures as had bike 20 years since college.

I am outraged, but I am sure I will find my bike, I am positive, please let me know guys if you see it.

title: 2011 Motobecane Nemesis (Pro)
description: 56 cm Glossy Black, Red and White 2011 Motobecane Nemesis Pro triathlon bike. The left shifter is slightly canted to the inside and the right brake is slightly canted to the inside as well. Superficial damage on seat post between 180-200 marks, Shimano 105 PD-5700 road pedals added along with bike computer attachment wrapped around right brake cable.  Shimano pedals are set to about 1/4 tension (loose). It has a very slight squeak with full weight.  The back tire is a Continental 4000 with a red stripe on the riding surface and the front tire is a Continental UltraSport worn down.  Right brake cable is slightly shorter than left brake cable and right shifter has scuff.  Left side's wrap is slightly loose and both aerobars have friction tape on the last inch or so of the wrap.  The Wrap is slightly worn.  The seat is adjusted to as far back as possible and the aerobars as far forward as possible. It has a cat's eye Velo 7 magnet attached to the front rim.  you can see a similar bike here. http://www.bikesdirect.com/products/motobecane/nemesis_pro_xi.htm

title: 2024 Trek Marlin 5 - Gen 2
description:
Stolen Trek Marlin 5 – very distinctive color and setup, not a stock build.

Frame color: Volt Blue → Miami Green fade (bright, high-visibility gradient that really stands out)
Rear pannier rack/bag attached
Hybrid clip-in pedals (clip-in + flat combo)
Handlebar-mounted phone holder
Center/top tube bag mounted just behind the stem
Front and rear lights installed
Bell + horn on handlebars
Side-view mirrors on BOTH sides (uncommon and highly noticeable)

This bike is extremely recognizable due to the bright blue-to-green fade and the number of accessories. It looks more like a commuter/touring setup than a typical mountain bike.

If you see anything matching this description, even partially, please reach out.

title: 2023 Sirrus Specialized Sirrus X 2.0
description:
Bicycle Description
	•	Brand/Model: Specialized Sirrus X 2.0
	•	Type: Hybrid / fitness commuter bike
	•	Frame: Aluminum, step-over (diamond frame)
	•	Color: Deep burgundy / maroon (matte finish)
	•	Wheels/Tires: 700c wheels with wider, semi-slick tires (designed for pavement + light gravel)
	•	Drivetrain: 1x drivetrain (single front chainring with multiple rear gears)
	•	Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes
	•	Handlebars: Flat handlebars (upright riding position)
	•	Fork: Rigid (no suspension)

Accessories/Identifiers:

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

Today, sometime between 9am and 5pm, it was stolen out of our driveway. Our security cameras somehow caught nothing.
Its black and dark gold, 21 speed hybrid bike with a water bottle holder and a detachable tire pump. The tire pump is mostly the dark gold color.
There are 2 identifying things on bike. Right hand shifter thumb control has a cracked mount with a piece of cover plastic missing. The water bottle mount has aftermarket webbing strap w/plastic clip-together buckle. Both can be seen in one of the pics.
